mentions.note: Part of 'Big Five Travel' theme; terrific trades ahead of earnings.
CEO Bastian reports strong travel and business travel demand despite $95 oil; real-world data beats Wall Street skepticism on airline margins.
Traders speculate on Iran peace rumors causing oil-driven volatility; Cramer warns this is pure guessing without edge, not investing; avoid endless on/off trading based on geopolitical noise.

Transitioning from cyclical boom-bust to secular growth winner via capacity discipline (no more aggressive expansion), premium focus, credit card partnerships (13% revenue). CEO/CFO signaling less oil-price hostage-taking. Up 28% YTD, fresh all-time highs. Multiple expansion thesis intact: currently 15x earnings vs. S&P 500 avg 22x; EPS growth $6 (2026) → $8.50 (2027) → $10 (2028). Buy on dips.
